quoteFirst: Though extremely tired, he sat up till
quoteLast: talking politics with Savelli, as Adele had foreseen, and when he was at last shown to his distant room by Bonifazio, who had spent most of his day in studying the topography of the castle, he was very nearly exhausted.
title: Pietro Ghisleri
author: Crawford, F. Marion (Francis Marion), 1854-1909

quoteFirst: One night, my present hostess, Mrs. H., and I prowled through some of them quite unattended, on our way back from a friend's dwelling, roused up the watchmen to unlock and unbar the gates, saw no other people astir, went down one of the water streets, hailed a boat, and were deposited close to the door of our own abode
quoteLast: ; such an event being quite of common occurrence in this quarter.
title: The Golden Chersonese and the Way Thither
author: Bird, Isabella L. (Isabella Lucy), 1831-1904

quoteLast: , as I should judge,     I heard my wife call my name quite distinctly and waked instantly     broad awake.
title: Apparitions and thought-transference: an examination of the evidence for telepathy
author: Podmore, Frank, 1856-1910

quoteFirst: Equally significant is the legend that when King Arthur had embarked at Southampton on his expedition against Rome,
quoteLast: he saw in a dream 'a bear flying in the air, at the noise of which all the shores trembled; also a terrible dragon, flying from the west, which enlightened the country with the brightness of its eyes.
title: Demonology and Devil-lore
author: Conway, Moncure Daniel, 1832-1907

quoteFirst: To-night I shall seek my couch
quoteLast: , and yet I'll warrant me I shall be the first stirring in the Abbey; and, in any case, I shall be in the saddle before thee."
title: The Lancashire Witches: A Romance of Pendle Forest
author: Ainsworth, William Harrison, 1805-1882; Gilbert, John, 1817-1897 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: , the Greeks dragged all their ships to land, and set fire to them, and burned them all, and broke up their camp, and went away flying.
title: Memoirs or Chronicle of the Fourth Crusade and the Conquest of Constantinople
author: Villehardouin, Geoffroi de, 1150-1213?

quoteFirst: On Christmas-eve, in this year, the lord Louis of Luxembourg, lord of Ligny, died,
quoteLast: at Lyon, and was very much regretted by the king and all who knew him, for he was universally beloved.
title: The chronicles of Enguerrand de Monstrelet, Vol. 12 [of 13]
author: Monstrelet, Enguerrand de, 1390?-1453; Johnes, Thomas, 1748-1816 [Translator]

quoteLast: I heard the spirit saluting the guests, and they saluting him; and I also noticed they hailed him as "relation," and then gravely preferred the request that he would "drive back the ship which had stolen his cousin."
title:
Old New Zealand: A Tale of the Good Old Times
And a History of the War in the North against the Chief Heke, in the Year 1845
author: Maning, Frederick Edward, 1812-1883

quoteFirst: _Ben_ & I drest ourselves pretty early with an intention to Breakfast with Colonel _Tayloe_, but the Servant who went with us was so slow in preparing that we breakfasted before we set out--We arrived at Colonel Tayloe's however by half after nine--The young Ladies we found in the Hall playing the Harpsichord--The morning cool with a fine Breeze from the North for I forgot to mention that
quoteLast: last Night a violent Gust of Blackness, Rain, & Thunder came on & gave us present Relief from the scorching Sun; there was no Dust & the riding was pleasant--The Colonel, his Lady, Miss Polly, Miss Kitty, Miss Sally, rode in their Great Coach to the Ferry--Distance about 4 miles--Ben & I on Horseback--From Colonel Tayloe's to this Ferry opposite to Hobbs's Hole the Land is levil & extremely good; Corn here looks very rank is set thick with Ears, & they are high & large, three commonly on
title: Journal and Letters of Philip Vickers Fithian: A Plantation Tutor of the Old Dominion, 1773-1774.
author: Fithian, Philip Vickers, 1747-1776; Farish, Hunter Dickinson, 1897-1945 [Editor]; Kredel, Fritz, 1900-1973 [Illustrator]
